Regression test for a switch statement with an object case in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple JavaScript script that demonstrates the use of a `switch` statement with a `true` condition. The `switch` statement evaluates the expression `true` and attempts to match it with the case `{"foo": "bar"}`. However, this case will not execute because the object `{"foo": "bar"}` does not strictly equal `true`. The script includes a `break` statement within the case block, which would terminate the `switch` execution if the case matched. This code serves as an example of how `switch` statements can be structured, although the specific case condition is not practical for execution.
